issues.title: Daily log missing legally-defensible fields
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/field.ts getDailyLogs maps only workers_onsite, total_hours, incidents, weather, summary. Missing: per-trade crew table, equipment log, material deliveries, visitors log, safety observations, toolbox talk, and immutable submission timestamp. Daily logs are legally discoverable evidence; missing fields create liability exposure.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/field.ts, expand the getDailyLogs mapper to include: crew_entries (array of {company, trade, headcount, hours}), equipment_entries (array of {type, count, hours_operated}), material_deliveries (array of {description, quantity, po_reference, delivery_ticket}), visitors (array of {name, company, purpose, time_in, time_out}), safety_observations (string), toolbox_talk_topic (string), incident_details (array), submitted_at (ISO timestamp, immutable), is_submitted (boolean). In src/components/dailylog/QuickEntry.tsx and src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, add form sections for each new field group using the 8px grid card layout. Add a 'Submit & Lock' button that sets is_submitted=true and displays submitted_at timestamp with a lock icon. After submission, all fields must be read-only; edits create a new audit version. Acceptance criteria: (1) all 8 field groups present in form, (2) submitted logs show lock icon and timestamp, (3) edit attempt on submitted log shows 'Create Amendment' prompt, (4) export PDF includes all fields in industry-standard format.

issues.title: Field capture lacks offline queue and sync status UI
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/field.ts getFieldCaptures reads from Supabase directly with no offline fallback. src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx has no visible sync indicator. On a jobsite with poor connectivity, captures silently fail or block. The platform promises offline-first field capture but has no visible proof of it in the field module.
issues.prompt: In src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx, integrate useOfflineMutation from src/hooks/useOfflineMutation.ts for all capture submissions. Import useOfflineStatus from src/hooks/useOfflineStatus.ts. Show a persistent sync status bar at bottom of the capture form: green 'Synced' when online, amber 'X items queued' when offline with item count, and a spinner during active sync. In src/hooks/useOfflineMutation.ts, ensure captures are stored in src/lib/offlineDb.ts with optimistic local state. In src/components/ui/OfflineBanner.tsx, add a tap-to-view action that opens a sheet listing queued items with type, timestamp, and retry status. Acceptance criteria: (1) capture works with no network, (2) queued count badge visible, (3) items auto-sync on reconnect, (4) sync errors show per-item retry button, (5) no silent data loss.

issues.title: Punch list missing two-step verification and progress metrics
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/field.ts getPunchList maps items but provides no completion rate calculation, no two-step verification state (sub marks complete, super verifies), and no before/after photo pair tracking. The module spec requires two-step verification as core quality control. Without it, closeout risk is unmanaged.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/field.ts getPunchList mapper, add: verification_status ('open'|'sub_complete'|'verified'|'rejected'), verified_by (string|null), verified_at (string|null), sub_completed_at (string|null), before_photo_url (string|null), after_photo_url (string|null), rejection_reason (string|null). In src/pages/PunchList.tsx, add metric cards at top: Total Items, Open, Sub Complete (pending verification), Verified, Completion %. In src/machines/punchItemMachine.ts, add states: open -> sub_complete (event: MARK_COMPLETE) -> verified (event: VERIFY) or rejected (event: REJECT) -> open. In src/components/forms/CreatePunchItemModal.tsx add verification role selector. In the list row, show two-step status badge using the standard color-dot system. Acceptance criteria: (1) state machine enforces two-step flow, (2) super receives notification when sub marks complete, (3) before/after photos shown side-by-side in detail view, (4) completion % card calculates from verified count only.

issues.title: AI photo analysis not wired into daily log or field capture flow
issues.description: src/components/safety/AIPhotoAnalysis.tsx exists but is isolated to the safety page. src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx and src/components/dailylog/PhotoGrid.tsx do not invoke AI tagging, captioning, or hazard detection on captured photos. The product vision requires one-tap photo with AI tagging as a core super workflow.
issues.prompt: In src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx, after photo capture call usePhotoAnalysis from src/hooks/usePhotoAnalysis.ts. Display AI-suggested caption in an editable text field pre-populated with the suggestion. Show detected tags (e.g., 'concrete pour', 'rebar', 'safety hazard') as removable chips. If analysis detects a safety hazard (confidence > 0.7), show a red SafetyAlert card using src/components/ai/generativeUI/GenSafetyAlert.tsx with one-tap 'Create Safety Observation' action. In src/components/dailylog/PhotoGrid.tsx add 'Auto-caption all' button that runs batch AI analysis. In src/lib/aiService.ts ensure analyzePhoto method accepts base64 image and returns {caption: string, tags: string[], hazards: string[], confidence: number}. Acceptance criteria: (1) AI caption pre-fills within 2s of capture, (2) hazard detection triggers safety alert UI, (3) tags are editable before save, (4) batch caption works on 10+ photos.

issues.title: Field capture has no QR code crew check-in flow
issues.description: src/workforce/QRCheckIn.tsx exists as a component but is not integrated into src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx or the daily log crew entry flow. The module spec and product vision both list QR/NFC crew check-in as a core field feature differentiating SiteSync from Procore.
issues.prompt: In src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, add a 'Crew Check-In' tab alongside existing capture modes. Import src/components/workforce/QRCheckIn.tsx. On check-in scan, resolve worker identity from src/api/endpoints/people.ts getDirectory, auto-populate trade/company, and append to today's daily log crew_entries via upsert. Show a live check-in board: trade name, company, headcount, checked-in time. Add 'Check Out' swipe action on each row. At end of day, crew_entries auto-total hours. In src/hooks/useCheckIn.ts add subscribeToCheckins(projectId) using Supabase realtime so the board updates live when multiple supers are checking in crews simultaneously. Acceptance criteria: (1) QR scan resolves worker in <1s, (2) check-in board updates live, (3) check-out calculates hours, (4) daily log crew table auto-populated, (5) works offline with queue.

issues.title: Daily log page missing metric cards and has generic empty state
issues.description: src/pages/DailyLog.tsx does not render metric cards at the top of the page (required by design standards for every page). The empty state when no logs exist shows generic fallback text rather than a helpful construction-specific prompt. This violates both the UI standards and founder P0 requirements.
issues.prompt: In src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, add a MetricCards row above the log list with: Today's Workers (sum of crew headcount from today's log), Man-Hours This Week (sum of total_hours last 7 days), Open Incidents (count of logs with incidents > 0 in last 30 days), Logs This Month (count). Each card uses the standard structure: Lucide icon (24px colored), label (12px muted), value (28px bold), trend vs prior period. Replace the empty state with: a HardHat icon (subtle gray, 64px), heading 'No daily logs yet', subtext 'Tap the button below to log today's work — weather, crew, equipment, and progress. Done in under 90 seconds.', primary CTA button 'Start Today's Log' that opens CreateDailyLogModal. Acceptance criteria: (1) metric cards show real calculated values, (2) empty state matches design spec, (3) empty state CTA opens modal, (4) metric cards visible at 768px+ breakpoint.

issues.title: Field endpoint mapper types use implicit any via spread operator
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/field.ts, all three mappers (getDailyLogs, getFieldCaptures, getPunchList) use spread of raw DB row (...l, ...p) combined with typed overrides, which leaks unvalidated DB columns into the return type as implicit any. getFieldCaptures returns raw data with no transformation or type assertion at all.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/field.ts, define explicit return interfaces: DailyLogEntry, FieldCapture, PunchListItem with all fields typed. Replace spread mappers with explicit field-by-field construction. For getDailyLogs return type, create interface DailyLogEntry {id: string; log_date: string; date: string; workers: number; manHours: number; incidents: number; weather: string; summary: string; crew_entries: CrewEntry[]; ...} mirroring the DB schema from src/types/database.ts. For getFieldCaptures, define FieldCapture interface and map each field explicitly. In src/types/api.ts, ensure DailyLogRow, PunchItemRow, FieldCaptureRow match current DB schema columns. Enable strict: true in tsconfig if not already set. Acceptance criteria: (1) no implicit any in field.ts, (2) return types fully specified, (3) tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors in this file, (4) all consumers of these functions get proper autocomplete.

issues.title: Punch list and field capture pages lack error boundaries and loading skeletons
issues.description: src/pages/PunchList.tsx and src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx have no skeleton loaders defined and no error boundary wrapping. When Supabase returns an error, the pages render blank. The design standards require skeleton screens matching content shape and specific error states with recovery actions.
issues.prompt: In src/pages/PunchList.tsx and src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, wrap each page in src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx. For loading states, replace any spinner usage with skeleton components: a row of 3 metric card skeletons (gray pulse rectangles 120px tall) followed by 8 table row skeletons (48px height, 100% width, with column-width-matching gray bars). Create src/components/field/PunchListSkeleton.tsx and src/components/field/FieldCaptureSkeleton.tsx. For error states, show: a construction-relevant icon, message describing what failed, and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). In src/hooks/queries/index.ts ensure usePunchList and useFieldCaptures hooks expose isLoading, isError, error, refetch. Acceptance criteria: (1) skeletons match final layout shape, (2) error state shows retry button, (3) ErrorBoundary catches render errors, (4) no blank page on any failure mode.

issues.title: Field capture camera and voice inputs have no ARIA labels or keyboard alternatives
issues.description: src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx camera trigger and src/components/field/VoiceCapture.tsx record button are icon-only buttons with no aria-label, role, or keyboard activation. Touch-only interactions with no keyboard path fail WCAG 2.1 AA Success Criteria 2.1.1 and 4.1.2.
issues.prompt: In src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx, add aria-label='Capture photo' to the camera button and aria-label='Upload photo' to the upload trigger. Add onKeyDown handler supporting Enter and Space keys. In src/components/field/VoiceCapture.tsx and src/components/field/VoiceRecorder.tsx, add aria-label='Start voice recording' / 'Stop recording' that updates dynamically based on recording state. Add aria-live='polite' region that announces 'Recording started', 'Recording stopped', 'Transcribing...', 'Transcription complete'. In src/components/field/CaptureTimeline.tsx, ensure each capture card has role='listitem', descriptive aria-label including type and timestamp. Run axe-core in test against these components. Acceptance criteria: (1) zero critical axe violations, (2) all interactive elements keyboard accessible, (3) screen reader announces recording state changes, (4) focus trap in capture modal.

issues.title: Weather auto-population not integrated into daily log entry form
issues.description: src/lib/weather.ts exists and src/components/dailylog/WeatherCard.tsx displays weather data, but src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx does not auto-populate weather fields from the API on load. Users must manually enter weather, which is error-prone and legally weaker than a timestamped API-sourced record.
issues.prompt: In src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx, on modal open call src/lib/weather.ts fetchWeatherForProject(projectId) which should accept project lat/lng from the project record. Auto-populate weather form fields: conditions (string), temperature_high (number), temperature_low (number), wind_speed (number), precipitation_probability (number). Display a 'Weather auto-populated from NOAA/OpenWeather' badge with the timestamp next to the weather section. Store weather_source: 'api' | 'manual' and weather_fetched_at: ISO timestamp in the DB row for legal defensibility. Allow manual override but flag override with weather_source: 'manual'. In src/lib/weather.ts, ensure fetchWeatherForProject returns typed WeatherSnapshot interface. Acceptance criteria: (1) weather fields populate within 3s of modal open, (2) source badge visible, (3) manual override possible, (4) source and timestamp persisted to DB, (5) graceful fallback if API unavailable.

invented_features.name: Predictive Site Readiness Score
invented_features.description: An AI engine that runs each morning and scores the site's readiness for the day's planned work (0-100) based on: weather forecast vs. outdoor activity schedule, crew check-in rate vs. planned headcount by trade, open RFIs affecting today's work scope, pending material deliveries, and equipment availability. Displayed as a single number on the Daily Log page with a tap-to-expand breakdown. If score < 70, AI generates a recommended mitigation plan automatically. No competitor has this.

invented_features.name: Legal Evidence Package Generator
invented_features.description: One-tap generation of a formatted PDF evidence package for any date range, combining: daily logs with weather API timestamps, photo log with GPS/time metadata, crew check-in records, incident reports, and AI-generated narrative summary. Output matches format accepted by construction claims attorneys. Designed for delay claims, dispute resolution, and insurance proceedings. Procore has no equivalent.

invented_features.name: Crew Productivity Pulse
invented_features.description: A real-time dashboard widget showing actual vs. planned headcount by trade, hours logged vs. budgeted for the week, and an AI-calculated productivity index per trade based on historical daily log data for that sub on this project. Flags trades running below 80% productivity with a suggested root cause (weather correlation, RFI blocking, coordination conflict). Updates every 15 minutes from check-in data.

competitive_intel: Fieldwire leads on mobile field capture UX and offline reliability but has zero financial integration and no AI. Procore has Daily Log and punch list but requires multi-tap navigation and desktop-class forms on mobile — a superintendent hates it. SiteSync's field module has stronger AI hooks and offline architecture than either competitor, but currently under-delivers on domain completeness: the daily log is missing legally-required fields that Procore actually gets right (per-trade crew counts, equipment logs, visitor records). The two-step punch verification Fieldwire pioneered is not yet implemented. Fix the domain depth gaps and SiteSync's field module surpasses both on every axis that matters to a superintendent.
summary: The Field Operations module has a solid architectural foundation — offline hooks, realtime subscriptions, XState machines for punch items, and an AI service layer — but critically under-delivers on construction domain completeness, which is the core promise to the superintendent user. The daily log is the most legally sensitive document in construction and its current mapper captures only 5 of the 12+ required field categories; missing per-trade crew entries, equipment logs, visitor records, and immutable submission timestamps creates genuine legal liability. The punch list lacks the two-step verification workflow that is standard in the industry and differentiates quality platforms. Mobile field capture has no visible offline queue status, meaning a superintendent on a spotty connection cannot trust their data is saved. AI photo analysis exists in isolation but is not wired into the capture or daily log flows where it would save 30+ seconds per photo. TypeScript quality has spread-based implicit any leaking through all three field endpoint mappers. Accessibility is the weakest dimension with icon-only buttons having no ARIA labels or keyboard paths. The weather auto-population exists in a library but is not connected to the daily log form, weakening the legal defensibility that is a core differentiator. Fixing the 10 identified issues, especially the daily log completeness and mobile offline experience, would move this module from a promising scaffold to a platform a real superintendent would choose over Procore.
